evidence_quote: Having had their outriders and scouts eliminated, the Lannister force is unaware of their commander's defeat or of Robb's army's approach, and is still divided in three camps in order to besiege Riverrun properly.
asserted_relation: The Battle of the Camps was fought in the three Lannister siege camps surrounding Riverrun

evidence_quote: It was Robb … and Brynden. Your brother is here as well, my lord.
asserted_relation: Robb Stark commanded the attack at the Battle of the Camps, leading two columns of armored horse to overrun the middle Lannister camp

evidence_quote: It was Robb … and Brynden. Your brother is here as well, my lord.
asserted_relation: Brynden Tully (the Blackfish) led Robb's van and attacked the north Lannister camp at the Battle of the Camps

evidence_quote: Lord Jon Umber burns the siege towers which were under construction, and Lord Blackwood releases the prisoners taken in the earlier battle under the walls of Riverrun, including Edmure.
asserted_relation: Jon Umber (the Greatjon) burned the Lannister siege towers during the Battle of the Camps

evidence_quote: It had been Lord Tytos who led the sortie that plucked her brother from the Lannister camp.
asserted_relation: Tytos Blackwood led the sortie from Riverrun that freed Edmure Tully and broke the Lannister middle camp from the rear

evidence_quote: It had been Lord Tytos who led the sortie that plucked her brother from the Lannister camp.
asserted_relation: Edmure Tully was held prisoner in the Lannister camp at Riverrun and was freed by Tytos Blackwood's sortie during the battle

evidence_quote: Lord Andros Brax leads his men from the middle camp to the rafts and attempts to make a crossing. Riverrun begins pelting the rafts with thrown rocks, however, and soon the rafts are flipping and sinking. Among those drowned is Lord Brax.
asserted_relation: Andros Brax drowned attempting to cross the river to reinforce the north camp during the battle

evidence_quote: Forley leads the retreat of two thousand spears and two thousand bowmen when he sees the battle is lost.
asserted_relation: Forley Prester commanded the southern Lannister camp and led the retreat when the battle was lost

evidence_quote: Having had their outriders and scouts eliminated, the Lannister force is unaware of their commander's defeat or of Robb's army's approach, and is still divided in three camps in order to besiege Riverrun properly.
asserted_relation: Jaime Lannister commanded the three-camp siege of Riverrun that became the Battle of the Camps; he was already captured at Whispering Wood before the battle itself and absent during it

evidence_quote: Grey Wind, Robb's direwolf, allegedly kills four men and a dozen horses.
asserted_relation: Grey Wind killed men and horses during the Battle of the Camps, fighting alongside Robb Stark

evidence_quote: I saw. Last night, when it began, I told them … had to see. They carried me to the gatehouse … watched from the battlements. Ah, that was beautiful … the torches came in a wave, I could hear the cries floating across the river … sweet cries … when that siege tower went up, gods … would have died then, and glad, if only I could have seen you children first. Was it your boy who did it? Was it your Robb?
asserted_relation: Hoster Tully watched the Battle of the Camps from the battlements of Riverrun, ailing and bedridden but carried to the gatehouse to witness
